basic_details.job_title: MPSC Group C Services Main Exam Notification Out - Apply Online for 1884 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Maharashtra Lok Seva Aayog (MPSC), CBD Belapur, Navi Mumbai – 400614
basic_details.post_name: Udyog Nirikshak, Kar Sahayak, Tantrik Sahayak, Duyyam Nirikshak (State Excise), Lipik-Tanklekhak
basic_details.department: Various - Industry Energy & Labour, Finance, Home, and State Government Offices
basic_details.short_information: MPSC is conducting Group-C Service Main Examination 2025 for 1884 posts across 5 categories with Graduation qualification, last date 04 June 2026.

important_dates.main examination date: Sunday, 12 July 2026 - Amravati, Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar, Nagpur, Nashik, Navi Mumbai & Pune (district centre-wise)
important_dates.document verification / joining date: To be notified after result declaration

application_fee.details: Fees are non-refundable. Bank charges and taxes are extra and borne by the candidate. Fee can be paid online (credit card, debit card, net banking via any payment gateway available on State Bank portal) or offline via challan at any branch of State Bank of India.

age_limit.details: Age is calculated as on 01-02-2026. Minimum age for all candidates is 19 years. Maximum age for General (Open) category is 38 years. Relaxation in upper age limit is as per Maharashtra Government rules for reserved categories.

qualification.education_qualification: Must have qualified Maharashtra Gat-Ka Seva Sanyukt Purv Pariksha – 2025. Result declared 04 May 2026. Non-qualified candidates cannot apply.
qualification.details: Candidates must have appeared in and qualified the Group-C Combined Preliminary Examination 2025 (result declared on 04 May 2026). Only prelim-qualified candidates are eligible to apply for the Main Examination. Educational qualifications must be held as on the last date for application (04-06-2026).

vacancy_details.Department: Udyog Urja va Kamgar Vibhag (Industry Energy & Labour Dept.)

vacancy_details.Post Name: Tantrik Sahayak, Vima Sanchanalaya (Technical Assistant, Insurance Directorate)
Duyyam Nirikshak, Rajya Utpadan Shulk (Sub-Inspector, State Excise)

vacancy_details.Department: Shasanache Vividh Vibhag (Various State Government Departments)

vacancy_details.Description: OA (One Arm), BA (Both Arms), OL (One Leg), BL (Both Legs), OAL (One Arm One Leg), SD/SI (Spinal Deformity/Injury), CP (Cerebral Palsy), LC (Leprosy Cured), Dw (Dwarfism), AAV (Acid Attack Victim), MDy (Muscular Dystrophy)
ASD-M/MoD (Autism Spectrum Disorder), SLD (Specific Learning Disability), ID (Intellectual Disability), MI (Mental Illness)
MD involving any combination of categories (1) to (4) above. Post-wise eligible PwBD sub-categories are detailed in Appendix-2 of the notification.

category_wise_vacancy.Description: MD involving any combination of categories (1) to (4) above. Post-wise eligible PwBD sub-categories are detailed in Appendix-2 of the notification.

salary_details.pay_scale: As per Maharashtra Government pay rules applicable to respective post/service

selection_process: Selection is through a written Main Examination (400 marks
2 papers) with negative marking. Prelim-qualified candidates who apply for the Main Exam will be considered. Final selection is based on merit in the Main Exam
category-wise
post-wise
combined with preference order (especially for Lipik-Tanklekhak). Document verification follows shortlisting.
Stage 1 - Main Written Examination (Mukhya Pariksha)
The Main Examination consists of 2 papers (total 400 marks). Paper 1: Marathi (50 Qs
100 marks) and English (50 Qs
100 marks) - Baaravi / 12th standard level
medium Marathi and English
objective multi-choice
1 hour. Paper 2: General Studies and Intelligence Test (100 Qs
200 marks) - Padvi / graduation level
1 hour. Negative marking: 25% (1/4 mark) deducted per wrong answer. If more than one answer is given for a question
it is treated as wrong and 25% marks deducted. If final marks fall below the minimum cut-off after deductions
the candidate's marks are calculated as zero (not negative).
Stage 2 - Merit List and Preference Order (Lipik-Tanklekhak)
For all posts: a General Merit List (Sarvasadharan Gunvatta Yadi) is prepared based on total marks in the Combined Main Exam
post-wise. For Lipik-Tanklekhak: after the General Merit List is published

all candidates on the list must re-submit their Appointing Authority-wise preference order within the stipulated period through the MPSC online portal. If preference order is not submitted within the deadline
it will be treated as if no preference was given. Final Recommendation List is prepared considering the preference orders submitted.
Stage 3 - Document Verification (Pramanpatra Padataalni)
Shortlisted candidates are called for document verification before or after the written exam result

as decided by MPSC. Original documents along with self-attested photocopies must be produced. Only documents claimed in the application will be verified. Documents not produced at verification will not be accepted later and no extension will be given. Candidates failing to appear for verification will be treated as not qualified. Final recommendation/appointment is subject to verification of all original documents by the appointing authority.
Stage 4 - Physical / Medical Examination (Duyyam Nirikshak Only)
Recommended candidates for Duyyam Nirikshak
Rajya Utpadan Shulk post must meet prescribed physical standards and will be medically examined by the government-appointed competent authority before appointment.

exam_pattern.details: The Main Examination covers all 5 posts under a common written examination. Post-wise separate result processes apply. All questions are objective multiple choice. Negative marking of 25% per wrong answer applies.

how_to_apply: Candidates shall apply online through the MPSC portal (https://mpsconline.gov.in) only. No other mode of application will be accepted. Before applying, candidates must ensure they have qualified the Group-C Prelim 2025 and completed KYC on the MPSC portal. Detailed application instructions are available at https://mpsconline.gov.in and https://mpsc.gov.in.
Documents Required to be Scanned and Uploaded (PDF format, min 50KB – max 400KB each):
Proof of name at birth (SSC / equivalent certificate)
Age proof
Educational qualification certificates
Social category (Magasavargiya) certificate
Economically Weaker Section (EWS) certificate (Financial Year 2025-26)
Valid Non-Creamy Layer (Non-Kimiyer) certificate (Financial Year 2025-26)
PwBD (Divyang) certificate from competent authority
Ex-Servicemen (Maaji Sainik) certificate
Sports reservation eligibility proof - Sports Certificate required at document verification
Orphan (Anath) reservation certificate
Project Affected (Prakalpagrast) certificate
Earthquake Affected (Bhookampgrast) certificate
Part-time employee (Anshkalin Padvi) certificate
Category change / inter-category claim certificate (Araakhiv Mahila, Magasavargiya, etc.)
Proof of SC/ST name change if applicable
Marathi language knowledge proof
Small Family Declaration (Laghan Kutumbache Pratidnya)
Typewriting certificate (Marathi and/or English - for Kar Sahayak and Lipik-Tanklekhak as applicable)
Work experience certificate (if applicable for relevant post)
Documents to be Produced at Document Verification:
All original documents as listed above (plus self-attested photocopies)
Printout of submitted application form
Valid photo identity proof (Aadhaar / PAN Card / Passport / Smart Card issued by DGA - original Aadhaar OTP or e-Aadhaar with colour printed photo mandatory)
If name changed (marriage etc.) - notarized affidavit and relevant certificate from Marriage Registrar or Gazette Officer
Sports certificate originals for sports quota candidates (verified by Sports Authority)
Any other document claimed in the application

Documents not submitted at the time of application cannot be added later. Candidates who do not produce originals at document verification will be disqualified and no extension will be granted under any circumstances.
Apply Online at: https://mpsconline.gov.in → Login / Register → Advertisements → Advertisement No. 014/2026 → View → Check Eligibility → Apply
For queries: Email: [email protected] | Phone: 022-69385900 | All revisions/corrigendum (if any) will be hosted on the official MPSC website only - candidates should visit regularly.
important_instructions: Only candidates who have qualified the Maharashtra Gat-Ka Seva Sanyukt Purv Pariksha – 2025 (result declared 04 May 2026) are eligible to apply for this Main Exam. Non-qualified prelim candidates' applications will not be accepted. All reserved category (SC/ST/OBC/NT/DT/SBC) and other horizontal reservation claims (PwBD, Ex-Servicemen, Sports, Orphan, Project-Affected etc.) must be properly supported by valid documents; category claims cannot be changed after application submission under any circumstances. For Lipik-Tanklekhak post, Appointing Authority-wise preference order must be submitted at the time of application. After merit list is published, candidates will again need to re-confirm preference order within the stipulated window - failure to do so will forfeit their allocation right. Candidates must mandatorily select options (Vikalp) for all posts they wish to be considered for. Posts not selected during application will not be considered, even if the candidate is otherwise eligible. Negative marking applies: 25% (1/4) marks deducted for each wrong answer. Multiple answers to a single question also count as wrong. KYC completion on MPSC portal is mandatory before applying (per order ADR-2425/SIR.06/2025/MA.T., dated 20 June 2025). Application fee is non-refundable. After payment, fee cannot be adjusted for any other exam or selection process. Admit card (Hall Ticket) will be available on MPSC online portal 7 days before the exam. Candidates must download and bring it to the exam. Candidates must bring their own identity proof (Aadhaar / PAN / Passport / Smart Card) to the exam centre. Only original Aadhaar with OTP or colour-printed e-Aadhaar (with photo) is accepted - Aadhaar-based identity proof alone is valid without other documents. Once exam centre (district) is selected, it cannot be changed. Requests for centre change will not be entertained under any circumstances. Documents not uploaded at the time of application cannot be submitted later and no extension will be given. Candidates failing to produce originals at document verification will be disqualified. The entire recruitment process will be governed by applicable Maharashtra service entry rules for each respective post/department (as amended from time to time and as per MPSC / State Government circulars). If at any stage it is found that a candidate has given false information, hidden relevant facts, or submitted forged documents, their candidature will be cancelled and legal action will be taken. All corrigenda and updates will be published on the official MPSC website only - candidates must check regularly.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for MPSC Group-C Main Exam 2025?
faq.answer: The last date to apply online is 04 June 2026 (11:59 PM), while challan payment can be done till 06 June 2026.

faq.question: Q2. Who is eligible for MPSC Group-C Main Examination 2025?
faq.answer: Only candidates who qualified the Group-C Preliminary Exam 2025 (held on 11 January 2026, result on 04 May 2026) are eligible.

faq.question: Q3. How many vacancies are available in MPSC Group-C Recruitment 2025?
faq.answer: A total of 1884 vacancies have been announced across 5 posts.

faq.question: Q4. What is the exam date for MPSC Group-C Main Exam 2025?
faq.answer: The main examination will be conducted on 12 July 2026.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for MPSC Group-C 2025?
faq.answer: The age limit is 19 to 38 years for General category candidates as on 01 February 2026, with relaxation for reserved categories.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for MPSC Group-C Main Exam 2025?
faq.answer: Selection is based on Main Written Exam (400 marks), followed by merit list and document verification.

faq.question: Q7. Is there negative marking in MPSC Group-C Main Exam?
faq.answer: Yes, 25% (1/4th) marks are deducted for each wrong answer.

faq.question: Q8. What is the exam pattern for MPSC Group-C Main Exam 2025?
faq.answer: The exam consists of 2 papers: Paper 1 (Marathi & English – 200 marks) and Paper 2 (General Studies & Intelligence – 200 marks).
